revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Iwatsutsukowake-jinja]][[ja:石都々古和気神社]] {{Expand Japanese|石都々古和気神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iwatsutsukowake Shrine | native_name = 石都々古和気神社 | image = Iwatsutsukowake Shrine.jpeg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iwatsutsukowake Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|37.145054|140.451019}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|es|Ajisukitakahikone|fr|Ajisukitakahikone|hu|Adzsiszukitakahikone|it|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|mni|ꯑꯖꯤꯁꯨꯀꯤꯇꯀꯍꯤꯀꯣꯅꯦ|ru|Адзи-суки-така-хико-нэ|simple|Ajisukitakahikone|zh|味耜高彥根神|qid=Q506610|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} | website = {{Official website|http://www13.plala.or.jp/seihuukan/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iwatsutsukowake Shrine'''|石都々古和気神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần ნინა|yi|יאפאניש|yo|Èdè Japaní|za|Vah Nyiedbonj|zgh|ⵜⵓⵜⵍⴰⵢⵜ ⵜⴰⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏⵜ|zh_classical|日語|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún-gí|zh_yue|日文|zh|日语|zhbooks|日语|zhvoyage|日语会话手册|zu|IsiJapani|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Iwatsutsukowake Shrine.jpeg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|es|Ajisukitakahikone|fr|Ajisukitakahikone|hu|Adzsiszukitakahikone|it|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|mni|ꯑꯖꯤꯁꯨꯀꯤꯇꯀꯍꯤꯀꯣꯅꯦ|ru|Адзи-суки-така-хико-нэ|simple|Ajisukitakahikone|zh|味耜高彥根神|qid=Q506610|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182044 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == X (Twitter) username (P2002) == * 10225jinja == Instagram username (P2003) == * iwastustukowake == Facebook username (P2013) == * iwatutuko == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 8036889 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4380005005267 == Commons category (P373) == * Iwatsutsukowake-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県石川郡石川町下泉269', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Iwatsutsukowake-jinja}} [[JA:石都々古和気神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11587235}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Iwatsutsukowake Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Iwatsutsukowake Shrine.jpeg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District|Q1155869|qid=Q1155869|1=Ishikawa District, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa, Fukushima|Q1205335|qid=Q1205335|1=Ishikawa, Fukushima}}Shimonoizumi 269 | 位置={{Coord|37|8|41.9|N|140|27|3.2|E|scale:5000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Iwatsutsukowake Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}（small）
claimed Mutsu Ichinomiya
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭=Second Saturday of September | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Iwatsutsukowake
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Iwatsutsukowake Shrine'''（いわつつこわけじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa, Fukushima|Q1205335|qid=Q1205335|1=Ishikawa, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District|Q1155869|qid=Q1155869|1=Ishikawa District, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It claims to be the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}（あじすきたかひこねのみこと） ; Associated deities * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}（おおくにぬしのみこと） * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}（ほんだわけのみこと、{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） The original deity is Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto. The same deity is enshrined at {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsutsukowake Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Tanagura|Q1155858|qid=Q1155858|1=Tanagura, Fukushima}}, and there is a theory this shrine was divided from there, though no clear proof exists. === History === This shrine stands on the summit of a mountain called Hachiman-yama. Its founding date is unknown, but many sacred rocks remain on the mountain, indicating it had long been a ritual site. It is regarded as a candidate for "Iwatsutsukowake Shrine in Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, which is its first appearance in written records. In Eishō 6 ({{ill|lt=1051|Q19363|qid=Q19363|1=1051}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Aritsura|Q11564122|qid=Q11564122|1=Minamoto no Arimitsu}} joined {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Q32613|qid=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} in suppressing the Abe clan and, for merit, received Ishikawa lands as steward. In Kōhei 6 ({{ill|lt=1063|Q19400|qid=Q19400|1=1063}}), he moved here, took the name Ishikawa Aritsura, and built Mitsuashi Castle (Ishikawa Castle) east of this shrine on Hachiman-yama summit. For 25 generations over 528 years this was the seat of the {{ill|lt=Ishikawa clan of Mutsu|Q11658603|qid=Q11658603|1=Ishikawa clan of Mutsu}}, until confiscated by Toyotomi Hideyoshi. In Jiryaku 2 ({{ill|lt=1066|Q19411|qid=Q19411|1=1066}}), a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|qid=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} (Hachiman deity and Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined together at Iwatsutsukowake Shrine. === Precincts === [[File:Iwatsutsu Kowake-jinja Komainu ac.jpg|thumb|Flying lion-dogs]] * Haiden * Honden * Gakuden * Shrine office * Temizuya * Earthen ramparts of Mitsuashi Castle site * Komainu of Iwatsutsukowake Shrine ("flying lion-dogs") Iwatsutsukowake Shrine honden.jpeg|Honden 石都々古和気神社参道の磐座 DQlVGUrVoAAb2nW.jpg|Sacred rocks along the approach 石都々古和気神社i石門 DQlVK6oVAAAL 0P.jpg|Stone gate === Rituals === The day Hachiman deity was enshrined together was the old calendar 9th month, 19th day, and since then the annual festival was held on that day. The {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q327532|qid=Q327532|1=Shintai}} carrying the sacred body descended from the summit to Umaba-chō temporary hall, where rituals such as {{ill|lt=nagare-yabusame|Q1886157|qid=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} were held for seven days. In 1975 (Shōwa 50), festival dates were changed to September 11-15 (new calendar). In 2003 (Heisei 15), the annual ceremony was moved to the second Saturday of September, with the prior three days as participation days. === Cultural properties === === Fukushima Prefecture designated cultural property === * Crocodile-mouth ornament of Iwatsutsukowake Shrine (fine craft) - a bronze ornament dedicated by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikawa Mochimitsu|1=UNKNOWN}} in Ōei 30 (1423), designated 1 October 1953[http://www.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/admin/hall/02/01/01/04.html Medieval cultural properties of Ishikawa Town] Ishikawa Town. === Ishikawa Town designated cultural properties === * One pair of Iwatsutsukowake Shrine komainu (sculpture) - a pair called "flying lion-dogs," made in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by stonemason {{ill|lt=Kobayashi Wahei|Q11461468|qid=Q11461468|1=Kobayashi Wahei}}, designated in May 2016[http://www.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/info/005321.html "Iwatsutsukowake Shrine komainu" newly designated as town cultural property] Ishikawa Town. * Yoshida Kōichi documents (old documents) - historical documents owned by Yoshida Kōichi, priest of Iwatsutsukowake Shrine and first mayor of Ishikawa Town; designated 25 March 1991. === Access === * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|Q11587873|qid=Q11587873|1=Iwaki-Ishikawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Suigun Line|Q1056202|qid=Q1056202|1=Suigun Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|qid=Q499071|1=East Japan Railway Company}}) From the precinct entrance to the honden is about a 10-minute mountain path. Separate from the approach, there is a road for cars up to beside the honden. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === External links === *{{twitter|10225jinja}} *{{facebook|iwatutuko}} *{{instagram|iwatsutsukowake}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iwatsutsukowake Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwatsutsukowake Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwatsutsukowake Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:石川町の建築物]] [[Category:石川町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]]
